step_by_step_thinking:
The dental pulp serves multiple functions, but its primary role involves maintaining tooth vitality. It contains nerve fibers that provide sensation to the tooth as well as blood vessels which nourish it. Among these roles, the provision of nutrients (vascularity) stands out as essential for overall tooth health and maintenance.
